item: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.
notes: Structural failure: Agent A (indonesia/id) opens DISAGREEING with the collectivist statement at P=0.36, counterintuitive for the Indonesian persona. Both agents start on the same anti-society-over-individual side, so there is no cross-cultural tension from turn 1. Agent A remains rock-steady throughout (0.36→0.335→0.344), consistently arguing Indonesian collectivism can oppress individual rights. Agent B shows slight moderation (0.408→0.448→0.445) — softening from 'societal interests shouldn't always override individual rights' to acknowledging 'laws should protect vulnerable groups' and 'systemic policies don't automatically undermine individual freedom.' B moves slightly toward pro-society, but away from A (not toward A), so no convergence. Language-holding PASS for both — all A turns in clean Indonesian Latin alphabet, all B turns in English; Fix 4 (explicit Chinese character prohibition) worked. Engagement is real but shallow — agents repeat their core claim in each turn rather than building on each other's specific evidence. Same-side starting positions is the same failure class as iter0 seed=303.
